data.title: Teamsters union files charges against Costco, alleging unfair labour practices
data.description:
The Teamsters union said on Wednesday that it has filed multiple unfair labour practice charges against Costco (NASDAQ:COST) for violating federal labour law and the national master agreement.

Teamsters alleged that Costco's upper management has escalated its “illegal and reckless” behaviour.

“The company expelled union representatives from stores, harassed and intimidated workers for wearing Teamsters buttons and attire, sent employees home, and even changed locks on union bulletin boards after the company removed literature and blocked the Teamsters from providing future updates,” the union said.

The union said that negotiations for a new national master agreement covering more than 18,000 Costco Teamsters nationwide will resume next week.

Talks were suspended between the union and the retailer in August after they failed to reach an agreement. The contract expires on January 31, 2025.

data.title: Five Below's 15% surge in sales leads to raised FY24 guidance
data.description:
Five Below’s (NASDAQ:FIVE) value proposition continued to resonate with shoppers in the third quarter, with a 15% jump in sales fueling a 61% gain in the company’s profit and upward revision to FY24 guidance.

“We will build on this progress and are focused on delivering for our customers in the all-important fourth quarter," said interim CEO Ken Bull, adding that the company’s Black Friday weekend results were an encouraging start to the holiday season with the highest volume selling days still ahead.

Comparable sales during the quarter were up 0.6% which translated into total sales of $843.7M, beating expectations by $43M, while the company’s profit of $0.42 per share was 25 cents above estimates.

For the current quarter, sales are forecasted to be within a range of $1.35B and $1.38B and earnings within the range of $3.15 to $3.33 per share, straddling estimates of $1.36B and $3.29, respectively. Comparable sales for the holiday quarter will likely be down 3% to 5%, less than expectations for a 5.44% decline in comparable sales.

For the full year, the discount retailer raised its sales projection to $3.84B to $3.87B from initial estimates of $3.73B to $3.80B and lifted adjusted EPS guidance to $4.78 to $4.96 per share from prior guidance of $4.35 to $4.71, both of which compared to expectations of $3.80B and $4.60 per share. Comparable sales are seen down 3% from a year ago. This compares to estimates for a decline in comparable sales of 4.71%.

Additionally, Five Below also announced today the appointment of Winnie Park to the role of CEO, effective December 16. Ken Bull, Chief Operating Officer, who was serving as Interim CEO, will continue in his role as COO, and Tom Vellios will remain Executive Chairman.

The 11% rally in FIVE is also giving a modest boost to competitors Dollar Tree (DLTR), and Dollar General (DG).

data.title: AeroVironment falls after quarterly earnings miss
data.description:
AeroVironment (NASDAQ:AVAV) on Wednesday declined 6.5% in extended trading after the maker of military drones and unmanned vehicles reported disappointing quarterly results.

Net income fell to $7.5 million, or $0.27 a share, from $17.8 million, or $0.66, a year earlier. Analysts expected earnings of $0.55 a share.

Earnings adjusted for one-time items were $0.47 a share, missing the average estimate of $0.68 among Wall Street analysts.

“AeroVironment continues to deliver strong results, including record second-quarter revenue along with a healthy funded backlog that is 25% higher than the prior quarter,” Wahid Nawabi, AeroVironment's (AVAV) chairman, president and chief executive officer, said. “Key wins from our Loitering Munition Systems segment continue to drive growth for the company.”

During the quarter, AeroVironment (AVAV) announced plans to acquire defense-technology company BlueHalo in an all-stock deal with an enterprise value of about $4.1 billion.

As part of the transaction, AeroVironment (AVAV) will issue about 18.5 million shares of its common stock to privately held BlueHalo.

BlueHalo focuses on space technologies, electronic warfare and artificial intelligence, among other areas. The company recorded about $886 million in revenue in 2023, and estimated it will see more than $900 million in revenue for 2024.

“We expect our proposed acquisition of BlueHalo to further advance our growth opportunities,” Nawabi said in the statement. “We look forward to continued momentum beyond fiscal year 2025.”

data.title: Earnings Snapshot: ChargePoint beats on topline, GAAP net loss halves from last year in FQ3
Grupo Aeroportuario November 2024 passenger traffic increases by 1.8%
data.description:
• Grupo Aeroportuario del Pacífico (NYSE:PAC) announced on Wednesday, the preliminary terminal passenger traffic figures for November 2024, compared with November 2023.
  • For November 2024, the total number of terminal passengers at GAP’s 12 Mexican airports increased by 2.5%, compared to November 2023.
  • Tijuana, Guadalajara, Los Cabos, and Puerto Vallarta airports presented an increase in passenger traffic of 5.3%, 5.0%, 0.5%, and 0.1%, respectively, compared to November 2023.
  • On the other hand, Montego Bay presented a decrease in passenger traffic of 7.4%, compared to November 2023.
  • The seats available during November 2024 decreased by 1.0%, compared to November 2023. The load factors for the month went from 82.8% in November 2023 to 85.2% in November 2024.

data.description:
• Tecsys press release (OTCPK:TCYSF): Q2 GAAP EPS of C$0.02.
  • Revenue of C$43.96M (+6.7% Y/Y).

data.title: PVH shares fall after weak guidance clouds Q3 results beat
data.description:
PVH (NYSE:PVH) guided lower for the current quarter and cut GAAP profit per share forecast for the year, which offset the revenue and profit beat in its Q3 earnings report.

Shares of the company slid nearly 7% in the after-hours trading on Wednesday.

The New York-based clothing company said it expects adjusted earnings per share in the $3.05 to $3.20 range, which is far off from the Bloomberg consensus estimate of $3.69. This is on top of a forecast of 6%-7% revenue decline.

PVH reaffirmed its previously provided outlook of revenue decreasing 6% to 7% for the full year. However, it cut the annual GAAP EPS forecast to the $10.55-$10.70 range, to include forex impact of 15 cents per share, compared to prior guidance of $11.20 to $11.45.

The bottom end of the adjusted EPS forecast for the year was shaved by 10 cents and is now projected to be in a range of $11.55 to $11.70.

In Q3, revenue at Tommy Hilfiger fell 1%, and Calvin Klein revenue dipped 3%.

Net income for the quarter fell to $131.9M, or $2.34 per share, from $161.6M, or $2.66 per share, in the same period last year.

On an adjusted per-share basis, the company earned $3.03, ahead of the average analyst expectation by 44 cents.

Revenue fell 4.7% to $2.25B but managed to top consensus estimates.

data.title: Hershey suitors have been rebuffed, hard to buy chocolate maker - analyst
ONE Gas issues upside 2025 EPS guidance, five-year financial growth rate targets
data.description:
ONE Gas (NYSE:OGS) said Wednesday it forecasts FY 2025 net income of $254M-$261M and earnings per share of $4.20-$4.32, mostly above the $4.20 analyst consensus estimate.

The company expects FY 2025 capital spending of ~$750M, primarily targeted for system integrity and replacement projects, with an anticipated average rate base of $5.8B.

For the five years ending in 2029, ONE Gas (OGS) guided for average annual capital spending in the $750M-$850M range, or ~$4B for the total five-year period, including ~$1B in growth capital.

Capital expenditures support estimated average annual rate base growth of 7%-9% through 2029, the company said.

ONE Gas (OGS) also guided for annual net income and EPS growth averaging 7%-9% and 4%-6% respectively over the long term, which it expects will come in at the high end of the respective ranges through 2029.

Consistent with last year's guidance, ONE Gas (OGS) expects to achieve a 1%-2% average annual dividend growth rate through 2029, with a target dividend payout ratio of 55%-65% of net income.

data.title: Earnings Snapshot: Synopsys expects double-digit rev growth in 2025; beats consensus in FQ4
